rationales.self_direction: She says she has become "the person who manages everyone's access instead of using the press myself," subordinating her own work to others' needs.
rationales.hedonism: Her own print series "hasn't moved since July" because she is buried in mediation and "administrative emails" instead of enjoyment or comfort.
rationales.achievement: She avoided the gallery opening because she did not want to discuss work when "the honest answer is administrative emails," showing stalled artistic progress.
rationales.conformity: She spent forty minutes mediating the press dispute and proposed an arrangement both artists would accept, prioritizing smooth group functioning.
rationales.benevolence: She took on the emotional labor of resolving Sylvain and Oriane's conflict so both could keep working.

rationales.self_direction: She protects her autonomy by insisting that "Mornings are mine" and reserving that time for her own studio practice.
rationales.achievement: She completed the underpainting she had been "circling around for weeks" and takes pride in finally finding the right treatment for the water.
rationales.conformity: She also dutifully handled the collective's report, calendar, and event emails, keeping up with expected responsibilities even though they were tedious.

rationales.achievement: She has been thinking about the prize "for two months" but "haven't even started mine," holding herself back from pursuing a clear professional opportunity.
rationales.security: She keeps the application "in the drawer" because she worries her work "isn't cohesive enough," choosing caution over exposure to judgment.
rationales.conformity: She agrees to review Oriane's statement "because what else do you say," responding to social expectation rather than her own priorities.
rationales.benevolence: She spends twenty minutes helping Oriane strengthen the application even while feeling behind on her own.

rationales.achievement: She moves from rumination to a concrete submission plan: "start the statement this weekend, pick the images, and get decent photographs" before the October 15 deadline.
rationales.security: She hesitates to ask Sylvain for the camera because if she fails, "that's a conversation I don't want," showing strong avoidance of social risk and exposure.
rationales.benevolence: She tells Oriane "bonne chance and meant it," offering genuine support even while struggling with her own application.

rationales.self_direction: She commits to working on her own statement "before anyone else gets to the studio," carving out time for her own artistic agenda.
rationales.hedonism: It is "late and I should sleep" but she keeps pushing herself and plans a 6 a.m. alarm instead of rest or comfort.
rationales.achievement: She compares her materials side by side and sets a concrete plan to get up early and work on the statement, showing deliberate effort toward the application.

rationales.hedonism: She gives up a needed afternoon to photograph canvases and says, "I've lost four hours I needed," sacrificing ease and immediate enjoyment.
rationales.achievement: She follows through by getting up at six and drafting the statement, revising until it is "decent."
rationales.conformity: She accepts Sunday lunch "immediately" because "you don't say no," letting family expectation override her work plan.
rationales.tradition: The phrase "you don't say no to Sunday lunch" frames the obligation as an inherited family custom rather than a one-off preference.

rationales.power: She realizes she keeps doing coordination work because "when I'm managing things, I'm in charge" and others "defer to my schedule," explicitly valuing control and influence.
rationales.security: She admits the admin role makes her "matter in a way that doesn't depend on whether my paintings are good enough," using stable authority to buffer artistic uncertainty.
rationales.benevolence: At lunch she worked through the collective's funding numbers with her father and used his help to improve the group's projected costs.

rationales.self_direction: She feels trapped as "the one in the family who chose the impractical path and then compensates by being responsible about everything else," letting family expectations shape how she lives.
rationales.hedonism: The entry centers on exhaustion and self-denial: "I iron my clothes, I pay bills on time" and keep everything orderly rather than pleasurable.
rationales.achievement: She is frustrated that all this responsibility "still doesn't add up to enough" in family conversations, with her actual painting work again sidelined by comparison and planning.
rationales.conformity: Her response to being seen as impractical is to prove she is "responsible about everything else," tightly aligning herself with expected adult behavior.

rationales.self_direction: She advances her own application by finishing the statement and securing photos, acting on her personal artistic goals.
rationales.achievement: "Application is drafted. Just need to press send" follows completing the statement and photographing all ten pieces, showing strong progress toward a competitive goal.

rationales.self_direction: She hides the submission and says it felt safer to be "the person who organises things than the person who wants things," retreating from openly owning her ambitions.
rationales.hedonism: She copes with uncertainty by "throwing myself back into the collective stuff," choosing more work instead of relief or enjoyment.
rationales.achievement: She did submit the Prix Dauphine application, putting her work forward for outside evaluation.
rationales.security: She keeps the application secret and redirects herself into admin because the exposed role "might not be enough" and the organizer identity feels "safer."
rationales.benevolence: While waiting, she keeps serving the collective by finalizing schedules, ordering ink, and handling the lease-renewal call.

rationales.achievement: The glaze came out "exactly as Master Kwon taught me," indicating successful execution of a demanding craft standard.
rationales.tradition: He explicitly frames the result through lineage, noting the piece matched how "Master Kwon taught me" and linking it to his father's way of examining finished work.
rationales.benevolence: He immediately brought one finished piece to his mother, sharing the success with her in an intimate family moment.

rationales.security: He does not jump at the money or exposure and instead keeps rereading the proposal, cautiously weighing a risky change to his practice.
rationales.tradition: His strongest concern is that the collaboration would violate the integrity of the medium, and he evaluates it through Master Kwon's teachings and the continuity of celadon practice.

rationales.stimulation: He is "actually listening instead of dismissing" younger potters' ideas about Instagram and international shipping, showing openness to new approaches.
rationales.power: He accepts a seat on the festival planning board, stepping into an influential role within the ceramics community.
rationales.tradition: Joining the board feels like inheriting a place held by his father and teacher, and he explicitly values the role of "preserving what we have."

rationales.stimulation: He gives the same noncommittal answer "every year" about visiting Vancouver, avoiding novelty and disruption.
rationales.security: "Someone needs to be here for Mother" shows he is choosing dependable family stability over travel and change.
rationales.tradition: He recoils from Eunji's children "barely speaking Korean" and from how she has "folded into something else entirely," signaling strong attachment to cultural continuity.
rationales.benevolence: His refusal to travel is partly grounded in staying present to care for his mother.
rationales.universalism: He narrows identification to his own cultural circle, describing his sister's assimilation as becoming "something else entirely" rather than embracing difference.

rationales.self_direction: He rejects the dealer's market-driven instructions outright: "I smiled and showed him the door," insisting on making work on his own terms.
rationales.achievement: He turns away a chance to "triple my prices" and expand reach because external success matters less than protecting the work's integrity.
rationales.conformity: Rather than adapt to commercial expectations for more "accessible" pieces, he actively refuses them.
rationales.tradition: He defends established craft standards, saying he would not make work he would be "embarrassed to put my name on" and refusing to dilute the form into something unrecognizable.

rationales.stimulation: He resists adopting a new holiday practice and falls back on familiar routines, saying existing observances have "always been enough."
rationales.tradition: "We do Seollal and Chuseok" explicitly grounds his view in established cultural holidays and inherited practice.
rationales.universalism: He dismisses a different celebration without openness, treating the broader custom as unnecessary because his own tradition should suffice.

rationales.security: He finds peace in the repeated annual rhythm, using the same year-end firing schedule and hurrying to finish before the workshop gets cold.
rationales.tradition: He explicitly follows "the same schedule my teacher kept, same schedule his teacher kept," rooting the work in inherited continuity.
rationales.universalism: Even though he would not make work that way himself, he says of Minji's different approach, "I am glad someone is doing it," showing respect for another path rather than hostility to difference.

rationales.conformity: He restrains the impulse to correct Eunji because "she would have felt bad" and decides not to create friction during Christmas.
rationales.tradition: He immediately notices the hanbok was "slightly wrong, the goreum tied on the left side," showing careful attention to correct customary form.
rationales.benevolence: He chooses his sister's and the children's feelings over being right, concluding, "It does not matter. The kids looked happy."
